key_points: An angle is formed when two rays meet at a common point called the vertex
The two rays forming the angle are called arms
Types of angles include acute, right, obtuse, straight and reflex angles
Complementary angles are two angles whose sum is 90°
Supplementary angles are two angles whose sum is 180°
Adjacent angles share a common vertex and common arm
A linear pair is a pair of adjacent angles whose sum is 180°
When two lines intersect, vertically opposite angles are equal
Parallel lines never meet and remain at a constant distance
examples: Vertically opposite angles example: opposite angles formed by intersecting lines
Corresponding angles example: equal angles when transversal cuts parallel lines
exam_tips: Remember that angles forming a linear pair always add up to 180°
Vertically opposite angles are always equal
Angles around a point always sum to 360°
If corresponding or alternate interior angles are equal, the lines are parallel
Use clear diagrams while solving angle problems
